title: 2. The Atom: Reproducible Data Generation
content:
2. The Atom: Reproducible Data Generation
This equation runs every time a new piece of data (an identifier) is created. It chains the new data to the user's previous action, ensuring a verifiable sequence.
* H_n: The New Atom's Hash. This is the unique, verifiable "fingerprint" of the new data.
* H_n-1: The Previous Atom's Hash (starting with H_local_genesis). This is the blockchain sequence, linking this new atom to the user's personal chain.
* S_user: The User Signature Seed. This ensures that only this user could have generated this specific hash.
* I_n: The Intent. This is the 4-element "metaphysical" data the user provides (e.g., Type='Process', System='bcd', Descriptor='norma').
* ID_n: The final, reproducible Identifier String (e.g., p-01-bcd-norma-pr0c3ss_).
* G(...): The Generator Function. This is the deterministic function that builds the identifier string.
* F_fibonacci: The Form. This is the immutable 1-2-3-5-8 structural rule.
How it Works: The "Atom" in Practice
This is how the equations create your reproducible identifier:
